openapi.description: This request retrieves a list of batches for operations and applications that have been performed on objects tracked by the system.

query_params.name: limit
query_params.description: Limit response to a specified positive number of objects. The maximum number of objects that can be returned is 100.

query_params.name: createdAfter
query_params.description: Exclusively filter records created after this timestamp.

query_params.name: createdBefore
query_params.description: Exclusively filter records created before this timestamp.

query_params.name: created
query_params.description: Filter by the Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when this object was persisted.

query_params.name: createdClient
query_params.description: Filter by the ID of the IMS client that created this object.

query_params.name: updated
query_params.description: Filter by the Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) for the time of last modification.

query_params.name: createdUser
query_params.description: Filter by the ID of the user who created this object.

query_params.name: updatedUser
query_params.description: Filter by the ID of the user who changed this object.

query_params.name: version
query_params.description: Filter by Semantic version of the account. Updated when the object is modified.

query_params.name: tags
query_params.description: Filter response by the specified tags.

query_params.name: status
query_params.description: Filter by the current (mutable) status of the batch.

query_params.name: recordCount
query_params.description: Filter by the total number of data records (rows or documents) processed in the batch.

query_params.name: failedRecordCount
query_params.description: Filter by the number of records that could **not** be processed in the batch.

query_params.name: size
query_params.description: The number of bytes processed in the batch.

query_params.name: started
query_params.description: Filter by the Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when the batch processing action was started. Subtracting "started" from "completed" will yield the total processing time.

query_params.name: completed
query_params.description: Filter by the Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when the batch processing action was completed. Subtracting "started" from "completed" will yield the total processing time.

query_params.name: startAfter
query_params.description: Filter for batches with availability dates that start after the specified timestamp.

query_params.name: startBefore
query_params.description: Filter for batches with availability dates that start before the specified timestamp.

query_params.name: endAfter
query_params.description: Filter for batches with availability dates that end after the specified timestamp.

query_params.name: endBefore
query_params.description: Filter for batches with availability dates that end before the specified timestamp.

query_params.name: batch
query_params.description: Filter on the ID of the batch.

query_params.name: externalId
query_params.description: Filter on the ID of the external object.

query_params.name: dataSet
query_params.description: Filter on the ID of the dataset.

query_params.name: orderBy
query_params.description: Sort parameter and direction for sorting the response. Ex. orderBy=asc:created,updated. This was previously called sort.

query_params.name: start
query_params.description: Specifies the offset of the pages of results. To get the first page of results, set the value to `start=0`.

query_params.name: property
query_params.description: Regex used to filter objects in the response.

query_params.name: properties
query_params.description: A comma separated allowlist of top-level object properties to be returned in the response. Used to cut down the number of properties and amount of data returned in the response bodies.

answer_constraints: Use only verified SQL/API outputs as evidence.
Do not invent IDs, timestamps, counts, statuses, or names.
If SQL and API disagree, mention the discrepancy clearly.
For count questions, prefer explicit total/count fields when available.
If API returns an error or empty result, state that clearly.
